heading: Limitations of time
label: 8298 Limitations of time
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6248.34 U.S.C. 360, ; renumbered § 9 and amended , .Feb. 4, 1919, ch. 14, § 740 Stat. 1056Aug. 7, 1942, ch. 551, § 156 Stat. 744The words “Except as provided in ” are substituted for the words “That except as otherwise prescribed herein” to identify the only exception. The words “may be awarded to a person” are substituted for the words “shall be issued to any person” for uniformity. The words “specific”, “or report distinctly”, and “at the time of” are omitted as surplusage. The words “bar, emblem, or insignia” are substituted for the words “or bar or other suitable emblem or insignia” for uniformity. The words “from the date of that act or service” are substituted for the word “thereafter”.section 6246 of this title

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 8298, acts , ; , , title IV, § 401(1), , 390, provided for promotion from grade of second lieutenant to first lieutenant after three years of service, discharge under upon failure of promotion, and filling vacancies for first lieutenants with second lieutenants, except Air Force nurses and medical specialists, prior to completion of three years of service, prior to repeal by , , , effective . See .Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 511Pub. L. 85–155, title III, § 301(9)71 Stat. 387section 8814 of this titlePub. L. 96–513, title II, § 20494 Stat. 2880section 630 of this titleAug. 21, 1957Dec. 12, 1980Sept. 15, 1981

Amendments2018—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232, § 807(b)(13)section 6248 of this titleSubsec. (a). , substituted “section 8296” for “section 6246” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–232, § 809(a)1960— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted “or subsection (b)” after “title”, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 86–582

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

children.text: the award is made within five years after the date of the act or service justifying the award; and

a statement setting forth the act or distinguished service and recommending official recognition of it was made by his superior through official channels within three years from the date of that act or service.

a statement setting forth the act or distinguished service and recommending official recognition of it was made by the person’s superior through official channels within three years from the date of that act or service and was supported by sufficient evidence within that time; and
no award was made, because the statement was lost or through inadvertence the recommendation was not acted on;
